summary: Testing strategy covers Parser well (6 scenarios) but has significant gaps: Writer module has test file in tasks but zero scenarios in strategy, Logger module has no tests at all, State Manager coverage is thin (2 scenarios for a module handling session persistence). The blanket 'no integration tests' rationale incorrectly groups standalone JS modules with n8n workflow testing — file writer and archive generator operate on real filesystem and deserve integration-level tests.

findings.issue: Writer module (src/writer.js) is listed in Task 6 with writer.test.js but has ZERO test scenarios defined in the Testing Strategy section. Writer handles file creation with automatic subdirectory creation, path traversal protection, and ZIP archive generation — all security-sensitive operations with acceptance criteria.
findings.recommendation: Add writer unit test scenarios: (1) write single file to new directory — file exists with correct content, (2) write file with nested path (e.g. 'assets/img/logo.png') — subdirectories created, (3) path traversal in file path (../../etc/passwd) — rejected, (4) write to read-only directory — error with descriptive message, existing files preserved, (5) ZIP archive contains all written files with correct structure, (6) empty file list — error or empty archive with clear behavior.

findings.issue: Logger module (src/logger.js, created in Task 7) has no test file and no test scenarios. Logger records prompts, responses, timestamps, retry counts — data used for retrospective analysis. Corrupted or incomplete logs silently break the retrospective workflow.
findings.recommendation: Add logger test scenarios: (1) log step entry — JSON contains step name, prompt, response, timestamp, (2) log multiple steps — all entries preserved in order, (3) log retry — attempts counter incremented, (4) read log from existing session — returns structured data. Create src/logger.test.js in Task 7.

findings.issue: State Manager has only 2 test scenarios (save/load roundtrip, missing file) for a module that manages complex session state with multiple fields (currentStep, currentFileIndex, fileList statuses, generatedFiles). Missing scenarios for state transitions, partial updates, and error resilience.
findings.recommendation: Add State Manager scenarios: (1) update currentStep and currentFileIndex — only specified fields change, rest preserved, (2) corrupted/invalid JSON file — graceful error or new session with warning, (3) update fileList item status from 'pending' to 'done' — correct item updated, (4) add entry to generatedFiles — previous entries preserved, (5) session with status 'completed' — prevent further modifications or warn.

findings.location: Section: Testing Strategy — Prompt template substitution
findings.issue: No test scenarios for the prompt template variable substitution logic that replaces {description}, {spec}, {previousFiles}, {templateContext} placeholders. This is data transformation logic that assembles context for LLM prompts — the core value proposition of the pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Add unit tests for prompt assembly: (1) template with all placeholders — all replaced with actual values, (2) template with {previousFiles} containing multiple files — all file contents included, (3) missing placeholder value — clear error or empty string with warning, (4) very large context (near LLM limit) — truncation or warning.

findings.issue: Blanket 'None — n8n workflow tested manually' groups standalone JS modules (writer.js, state.js, logger.js) with n8n-specific workflow testing. Writer and archive generator perform real filesystem operations that are ideal candidates for integration tests — they don't require n8n at all.
findings.recommendation: Separate the rationale: 'No integration tests for n8n workflow nodes (tested manually)' is valid. But add integration tests for writer.js (real filesystem writes, directory creation, ZIP generation with real archiver library) and state.js (real JSON file read/write cycles). These run without n8n and take seconds.

findings.location: Section: Testing Strategy — Template config validation
findings.issue: 12 template config JSON files are created in Task 2 with no validation tests. Missing or malformed configs would cause runtime failures in the workflow with no clear error message.
findings.recommendation: Add a simple validation test: load all 12 template configs, verify each has required fields (id, name, category, stack, maxFiles, typicalFiles, promptContext). This is a low-effort test that catches config typos early.
